---
title: "DNS trouble in Ubuntu Server"
date: 2011-04-08
forum: Server Platforms
---

### Post by cosborn72 on 2011-04-08
I recently set up Ubuntu 10.10 server on a machine at work.  I was able to set up apache and ssh without a hitch, but when I tried to set up Postfix, I continued to get errors trying to send messages.  I was also getting errors running apt-get.  

What I determined was that both were DNS problems.

I looked in /etc/resolv.conf, and found this.
```

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN

```

This is my /etc/networking/interfaces files
```
# The loopback network interface
auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

# The primary network interface
auto eth0
iface eth0 inet static
        address 170.215.112.100
        netmask 255.255.255.248
        gateway 170.215.112.97

```
Which I believe means I have the computer set up with a static IP.

When I put my nameserver information into resolv.conf, it disappears soon after.

I put my nameserver information in /etc/resolvconf/resolv.conf.d/ original file, but it didn't help.
```

nameserver 170.215.126.3
nameserver 170.215.144.199

```

Does anyone have any ideas? I can shh, access apache pages, and anything else involving connecting to the server, but the server can't send any requests out due to DNS errors.

---

### Post by falko on 2011-04-09
Do you use NetworkManager (is there such a script in /etc/init.d/)? If so, stop it (use /etc/init.d/networking instead). Afterwards, it should be possible to modify /etc/resolv.conf.

---

### Post by R.Bucky on 2011-04-09
You can stop resolv.conf from being overwritten with chattr ([http://nwlinux.com/how-to-prevent-etcresolv-conf-from-being-overwritten/]("http://nwlinux.com/how-to-prevent-etcresolv-conf-from-being-overwritten/")).

---

### Post by cosborn72 on 2011-04-11
Both solutions helped.  Thanks!

---
